result.stdout:
DotMR - A first-class monorepo management framework for .NET 10+

Usage: dotmr <command> [options]

Commands:
  init          Initialize a DotMR monorepo configuration
  validate      Validate monorepo structure against conventions
  graph         Query the project dependency graph
  viz           Generate interactive visualizations
  affected      List projects affected by changes
  build         Build projects
  restore       Restore NuGet packages for projects
  test          Run tests for projects
  clean         Clean build artifacts and cache directories
  format        Format code in projects
  slnf          Generate solution filter files
  open          Open solution or filter file in IDE
  ci            Generate CI/CD pipeline configurations
  version       Manage semantic versions for projects
  release       Coordinate multi-package releases
  generate, g   Generate new projects from templates
  publish       Publish NuGet packages in dependency order
  search        Search for projects in the monorepo
  boundary      Check dependency boundaries and code ownership
  report        Generate telemetry and metrics report
  plugin        Manage DotMR plugins
  migrate       Run migration recipes to refactor code
  dashboard     Manage dashboard lifecycle (start/stop/restart/status)
  mcp           Manage MCP server lifecycle (start/stop/status)
  completion    Generate and install shell completion scripts

Options:
  -h, --help       Show help information
  -v, --verbose    Enable verbose logging
  -V, --version    Show version information

Use 'dotmr <command> --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
List projects affected by changes

Usage: dotmr affected [options]

Options:
  -b, --base <ref>       Base git reference to compare against. Default: HEAD
  -c, --compare <ref>    Compare git reference (defaults to working directory)
  -f, --format <format>  Output format (text or json). Default: text
  -h, --help             Show help information

Examples:

  # Beginner: Check which projects are affected by uncommitted changes
  dotmr affected

  # Intermediate: Compare current branch against main branch
  dotmr affected --base origin/main

  # Intermediate: Compare two specific branches
  dotmr affected --base main --compare feature/new-api

  # Advanced: Get affected projects in JSON format for CI/CD pipelines
  dotmr affected --base origin/main --format json

  # Advanced: Check what would be affected by a specific commit
  dotmr affected --base HEAD~1 --compare HEAD

Common Workflows:
  - Pre-commit check:    dotmr affected
  - PR validation:       dotmr affected --base origin/main
  - Release planning:    dotmr affected --base v1.0.0 --compare develop
  - CI integration:      dotmr affected --base $CI_BASE_BRANCH --format json

result.stdout:
Build projects

Usage: dotmr build [options]

Options:
  -a, --affected              Build only affected projects based on git changes
  -b, --base <ref>            Base git reference for affected detection. Default: HEAD
  -c, --configuration <conf>  Build configuration (Debug, Release, etc.)
  -j, --parallel <count>      Maximum number of parallel build tasks
  --plan                      Show execution plan without building
  --no-restore                Skip restoring NuGet packages before build
  -q, --quiet                 Suppress progress output
  -v, --verbose               Show verbose progress output
  -h, --help                  Show help information

Options:

result.stdout:
Generate CI/CD pipeline YAML configuration

Usage: dotmr ci emit [options]

Options:
  -p, --platform <platform>       Target platform (github, azure, gitlab). Default: github
  -n, --name <name>               Pipeline/workflow name. Default: CI
  -o, --output <path>             Output file path
  --template                      Use a predefined template
  --template-name <name>          Template name. Options:
                                    default, full, minimal,
                                    package-publish, container, multi-repo
                                  Default: default
  --dotnet-versions <versions>    Comma-separated .NET versions (e.g., '10.0.x,11.0.x')
  --os <systems>                  Comma-separated OS list (e.g., 'ubuntu-latest,windows-latest')
  --tasks <tasks>                 Comma-separated tasks (build, test, pack). Default: build,test
  --branches <branches>           Comma-separated branch patterns. Default: main,develop
  --matrix                        Enable matrix builds across OS and .NET versions
  --optimize-for <strategy>       Optimize pipeline (speed, cost, balanced). Default: balanced
  --estimate                      Show resource estimates (duration, cost)
  --validate                      Validate YAML syntax before writing
  --dry-run                       Preview generated pipeline without writing to disk
  --diff                          Show changes when regenerating existing pipeline file
  --no-affected                   Disable affected project detection
  --no-cache                      Disable caching
  --no-parallel                   Disable parallelization
  -v, --verbose                   Show generated YAML
  -h, --help                      Show help information

Template Descriptions:
  default/basic       - Standard build and test pipeline
  full                - Complete pipeline with all features and multi-platform
  minimal             - Bare minimum pipeline for quick builds
  package-publish     - Pipeline for building and publishing NuGet packages
  container           - Pipeline for building and pushing Docker containers
  multi-repo          - Pipeline for repositories with multiple solutions

Examples:
  dotmr ci emit --platform github
  dotmr ci emit --platform azure --template --template-name full
  dotmr ci emit --platform gitlab --template --template-name package-publish
  dotmr ci emit --matrix --os ubuntu-latest,windows-latest --validate
  dotmr ci emit --optimize-for speed --estimate
  dotmr ci emit --optimize-for cost --estimate
  dotmr ci emit --dry-run --diff

Preview CI/CD pipeline changes and resource estimates

Usage: dotmr ci preview [options]

Options:
  -p, --platform <platform>       Target platform (github, azure, gitlab). Default: github
  -n, --name <name>               Pipeline/workflow name. Default: CI
  -o, --output <path>             Output file path to compare against
  -b, --base <ref>                Base branch for comparison. Default: main
  --dotnet-versions <versions>    Comma-separated .NET versions (e.g., '10.0.x,11.0.x')
  --os <systems>                  Comma-separated OS list (e.g., 'ubuntu-latest,windows-latest')
  --tasks <tasks>                 Comma-separated tasks (build, test, pack). Default: build,test
  --branches <branches>           Comma-separated branch patterns. Default: main,develop
  --matrix                        Enable matrix builds across OS and .NET versions
  --optimize-for <strategy>       Optimize pipeline (speed, cost, balanced). Default: balanced
  --no-affected                   Disable affected project detection
  --no-cache                      Disable caching
  --no-parallel                   Disable parallelization
  -h, --help                      Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr ci preview
  dotmr ci preview --platform github --base main
  dotmr ci preview --optimize-for speed
  dotmr ci preview --optimize-for cost
  dotmr ci preview --matrix --os ubuntu-latest,windows-latest,macos-latest

result.stdout:
Clean build artifacts and cache directories

Usage: dotmr clean [options]

Options:
  -a, --affected              Clean only affected projects based on git changes
  -b, --base <ref>            Base git reference for affected detection. Default: HEAD
  -p, --project <path>        Clean specific project
  --cache-only                Clean only .dotmr/cache directory, not build artifacts
  --artifacts-only            Clean only bin/obj directories, not cache
  --dry-run                   Preview what would be deleted without actually deleting
  -j, --parallel <count>      Maximum number of parallel clean tasks
  -q, --quiet                 Suppress progress output
  -v, --verbose               Show verbose progress output
  -h, --help                  Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr clean                 # Clean all projects (artifacts + cache)
  dotmr clean --affected      # Clean only affected projects
  dotmr clean --cache-only    # Clean only cache
  dotmr clean --dry-run       # Preview what would be cleaned

result.stdout:
Generate and install shell completion scripts

Usage: dotmr completion <shell> [options]

Shells:
  bash         Generate Bash completion script
  zsh          Generate Zsh completion script
  powershell   Generate PowerShell completion script
  fish         Generate Fish completion script
  install      Auto-detect shell and install completion

Options:
  -h, --help   Show help information

Examples:
  # Generate Bash completion script
  dotmr completion bash > ~/.dotmr-completion.bash

  # Generate Zsh completion script
  dotmr completion zsh > ~/.dotmr-completion.zsh

  # Generate PowerShell completion script
  dotmr completion powershell > $HOME\Documents\PowerShell\Scripts\dotmr-completion.ps1

  # Generate Fish completion script
  dotmr completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/dotmr.fish

  # Auto-install for current shell
  dotmr completion install

After generating the script, you need to source it or restart your shell:

  Bash:       source ~/.dotmr-completion.bash
  Zsh:        source ~/.dotmr-completion.zsh
  PowerShell: . $HOME\Documents\PowerShell\Scripts\dotmr-completion.ps1
  Fish:       Automatically loaded from ~/.config/fish/completions/

result.stdout:
Manage DotMR dashboard and MCP server lifecycle

Usage: dotmr dashboard <command> [options]

Commands:
  start      Start the dashboard (and MCP server by default)
  stop       Stop the running dashboard and MCP server
  restart    Restart the dashboard
  status     Show dashboard status
  logs       View dashboard logs

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help information

Use 'dotmr dashboard <command> --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
View dashboard logs

Usage: dotmr dashboard logs [options]

Options:
  -n, --lines <N>    Show last N lines of logs (default: all)
  -f, --follow       Follow log output (like 'tail -f')
  -h, --help         Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr dashboard logs                # Show all logs
  dotmr dashboard logs --lines 50     # Show last 50 lines
  dotmr dashboard logs --follow       # Stream logs in real-time
  dotmr dashboard logs -n 100 -f      # Show last 100 lines and follow

result.stdout:
Start the dashboard (and MCP server by default)

Usage: dotmr dashboard start [options]

Options:
  -p, --port <port>    Dashboard port (default: from config or random)
  --no-open            Don't automatically open browser
  --mcp-only           Start only MCP server, not dashboard
  --dashboard-only     Start only dashboard, not MCP server
  -h, --help           Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr dashboard start                 # Start both dashboard and MCP server
  dotmr dashboard start --port 5234     # Start with specific port
  dotmr dashboard start --no-open       # Start without opening browser
  dotmr dashboard start --mcp-only      # Start only MCP server
  dotmr dashboard start --dashboard-only # Start only dashboard

result.stdout:
Format code in projects

Usage: dotmr format [options]

Options:
  -a, --affected              Format only affected projects based on git changes
  -b, --base <ref>            Base git reference for affected detection. Default: HEAD
  --verify-no-changes         Verify formatting without applying changes (for CI)
  -p, --project <path>        Format specific project
  -j, --parallel <count>      Maximum number of parallel format tasks
  -q, --quiet                 Suppress progress output
  -v, --verbose               Show verbose progress output
  -h, --help                  Show help information

Examples:

  # Format all projects
  dotmr format

  # Format only affected projects since last commit
  dotmr format --affected

  # Format only affected projects since main branch
  dotmr format --affected --base origin/main

  # Verify formatting without changes (for CI)
  dotmr format --verify-no-changes --affected

  # Format specific project
  dotmr format --project src/DotMR.Core/DotMR.Core.csproj

Notes:
  - Respects .editorconfig settings in the repository
  - Exit code 0 = success, 1 = formatting needed (with --verify-no-changes) or failure
  - Uses 'dotnet format' under the hood for actual formatting

Examples:

  # Format all projects

  # Format only affected projects since last commit

  # Verify formatting without changes (for CI)

  # Format specific project

Notes:
  - Respects .editorconfig settings in the repository
  - Exit code 0 = success, 1 = formatting needed (with --verify-no-changes) or failure
  - Uses 'dotnet format' under the hood for actual formatting

result.stdout:
ℹ Generate new projects from templates
ℹ 
ℹ Usage: dotmr generate <type> [name] [options]
ℹ        dotmr g <type> [name] [options]
ℹ 
ℹ Project Types:
ℹ   app, application    Generate a console application project
ℹ   lib, library        Generate a class library project
ℹ   test                Generate a test project
ℹ 
ℹ Options:
ℹ   -n, --name <name>        Project name (can also be first positional argument)
ℹ   -o, --output <path>      Output directory (default: src/ or tests/)
ℹ   -f, --framework <tfm>    Target framework (default: net10.0)
ℹ   -r, --reference <refs>   Comma-separated list of project references
ℹ   --force                  Overwrite existing files
ℹ   --skip-solution          Do not add project to solution
ℹ   -h, --help               Show help information
ℹ 
ℹ Examples:
ℹ   dotmr g app MyApp                           # Create console app in src/MyApp
ℹ   dotmr g lib MyLib -o libs                   # Create library in libs/MyLib
ℹ   dotmr g test MyLib.Tests -r MyLib           # Create test project with reference
ℹ   dotmr generate app MyApp --framework net11.0  # Create app targeting .NET 11

result.stdout:
Query the project dependency graph

Usage: dotmr graph [options]

Options:
  -p, --project <path>         Project to query (displays dependencies and dependants)
  -l, --list-all               List all projects in the graph
  -e, --export <path>          Export graph to file
  --export-format <format>     Export format: json, dot, markdown (auto-detected from file extension)
  -f, --format <format>        Output format (text or json). Default: text
  -h, --help                   Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr graph --list-all                   List all projects
  dotmr graph -p MyProject.csproj          Show project dependencies
  dotmr graph --export graph.json          Export graph to JSON
  dotmr graph --export graph.dot           Export graph to DOT format
  dotmr graph --export graph.md            Export graph to Markdown

commands.payload:
Query the project dependency graph

Usage: dotmr graph [options]

Options:
  -p, --project <path>         Project to query (displays dependencies and dependants)
  -l, --list-all               List all projects in the graph
  -e, --export <path>          Export graph to file
  --export-format <format>     Export format: json, dot, markdown (auto-detected from file extension)
  -f, --format <format>        Output format (text or json). Default: text
  -h, --help                   Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr graph --list-all                   List all projects
  dotmr graph -p MyProject.csproj          Show project dependencies
  dotmr graph --export graph.json          Export graph to JSON
  dotmr graph --export graph.dot           Export graph to DOT format
  dotmr graph --export graph.md            Export graph to Markdown

result.stdout:
Run migration recipes to refactor code

Usage:
  dotmr migrate <recipe-id-or-file> [options]
  dotmr migrate --list
  dotmr migrate info <recipe-id>

Arguments:
  <recipe-id-or-file>      Recipe ID or path to recipe file (YAML or JSON)

Commands:
  --list, -l               List all available recipes
  info <recipe-id>         Show detailed information about a recipe

Options:
  --dry-run, --preview     Preview changes without applying them
  --continue-on-error      Continue executing steps even if errors occur
  --no-validate            Skip validation after transformation
  --backup                 Create backup before applying changes
  -j, --parallel <count>   Maximum degree of parallelism (default: 1)
  -p, --projects <list>    Comma-separated list of target projects
  -h, --help               Show help information

Examples:

  # Beginner: List all available migration recipes
  dotmr migrate --list

  # Beginner: Get detailed information about a recipe
  dotmr migrate info rename-namespace

  # Beginner: Preview changes before applying (safe dry-run)
  dotmr migrate rename-namespace --dry-run

  # Intermediate: Run a migration recipe on specific projects
  dotmr migrate rename-symbol --projects src/Core,src/Api

  # Intermediate: Run migration with backup for safety
  dotmr migrate update-using-directive --backup

  # Advanced: Run custom recipe file with parallel execution
  dotmr migrate ./custom-recipe.yml --parallel 4

  # Advanced: Force migration to continue even if some files fail
  dotmr migrate rename-namespace --continue-on-error --no-validate

  # Advanced: Combine dry-run with project filtering for large repos
  dotmr migrate update-dependency --projects src/* --dry-run --parallel 8

Common Workflows:
  - Safe exploration:       dotmr migrate --list && dotmr migrate info <id>
  - Test before apply:      dotmr migrate <recipe> --dry-run
  - Safe migration:         dotmr migrate <recipe> --backup
  - Fast large-scale:       dotmr migrate <recipe> --parallel 8
  - Targeted refactor:      dotmr migrate <recipe> -p <projects> --backup

Migration Patterns:
  - Namespace rename:       Use rename-namespace recipe
  - Symbol rename:          Use rename-symbol for types/methods
  - Update imports:         Use update-using-directive recipe
  - Custom transform:       Create YAML/JSON recipe file

  -h, --help              Show help information

  dotmr open --file MySolution.slnx --ide Rider

result.stdout:
Usage: dotmr publish [options] [projects...]

Publishes NuGet packages in dependency order.

Options:
  -f, --feed <name>         Target feed name (uses first enabled feed if not specified)
  --skip-build              Skip building projects before packing
  --dry-run                 Show what would be published without actually publishing
  --validate                Validate package versions without publishing
  --generate-config         Generate NuGet.config with configured feeds
  --config-output <path>    Output path for generated NuGet.config (default: ./NuGet.config)
  -h, --help                Show help information

Arguments:
  projects                  Paths to specific projects to publish (publishes all if not specified)

Examples:
  dotmr publish                              Publish all packable projects
  dotmr publish src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj       Publish specific project
  dotmr publish --feed MyFeed --dry-run      Preview publishing to specific feed
  dotmr publish --validate                   Validate package versions
  dotmr publish --generate-config            Generate NuGet.config

result.stdout:
Usage: dotmr release [options]

Options:
  -p, --projects <names>      Comma-separated project names (all if not specified)
  -s, --strategy <strategy>   Version bump strategy: patch, minor, major (default: patch)
  -r, --pre-release <label>   Pre-release label (e.g., alpha, beta, rc.1)
  -d, --dry-run               Preview changes without modifying files
  --changelog-only            Generate changelog only, without version changes
  --no-tag                    Skip creating Git tags
  --no-commit                 Skip committing version changes
  -h, --help                  Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr release --strategy minor
  dotmr release --projects MyProject --strategy major --pre-release beta
  dotmr release --dry-run
  dotmr release --changelog-only

result.stdout:
Generate telemetry and metrics report

Usage: dotmr report [options]

Options:
  -f, --format <format>     Output format: json, prometheus, appinsights (default: json)
  -o, --output <path>       Output file path (default: stdout)
  --include-cache           Include cache statistics in the report
  --historical              Query historical telemetry data (requires persistence enabled)
  --recent <count>          Show most recent N reports (default: 10)
  --since <date>            Show reports since date (ISO 8601 format)
  --until <date>            Show reports until date (ISO 8601 format)
  -h, --help                Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr report                                    # Display current session JSON report
  dotmr report --format prometheus                # Display Prometheus metrics
  dotmr report --output metrics.json              # Save JSON report to file
  dotmr report --historical --recent 5            # Show last 5 historical reports
  dotmr report --historical --since 2024-01-01    # Show reports since Jan 1, 2024
  dotmr report --recent 10 -o historical.json     # Save last 10 reports to file

Note: Historical queries require telemetry persistence to be enabled in configuration.
      Set 'telemetry.enablePersistence = true' in dotmr.json to enable persistence.

Note: Historical queries require telemetry persistence to be enabled in configuration.
      Set 'telemetry.enablePersistence = true' in dotmr.json to enable persistence.

result.stdout:
Restore NuGet packages for projects

Usage: dotmr restore [options]

Options:
  -a, --affected              Restore only affected projects based on git changes
  -b, --base <ref>            Base git reference for affected detection. Default: HEAD
  -c, --configuration <conf>  Configuration (Debug, Release, etc.)
  -j, --parallel <count>      Maximum number of parallel restore tasks
  -f, --force                 Force restore by clearing NuGet cache and re-downloading packages
  --plan                      Show execution plan without restoring
  -q, --quiet                 Suppress progress output
  -v, --verbose               Show verbose progress output
  -h, --help                  Show help information

result.stdout:
Search for projects in the monorepo

Usage: dotmr search <query> [options]

Arguments:
  <query>                     Search query to match project names or paths
                              Supports glob-style wildcards:
                                * matches any characters
                                ? matches a single character
                                [abc] matches any character in the set

Options:
  -r, --regex                 Use regex pattern matching instead of glob
  -t, --include-tests         Include test projects in results (excluded by default)
  -e, --exact                 Require exact name match instead of substring match
  -x, --exclude <pattern>     Exclude projects matching pattern (supports multiple)
  -s, --sort <field>          Sort results by: name (default), references, or path
  -d, --descending            Sort in descending order (default is ascending)
  -f, --format <format>       Output format (text, json)
  --show-history              Show recent search history
  --clear-cache               Clear the cached dependency graph
  -h, --help                  Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr search core                           Search for projects with 'core' in the name
  dotmr search Core --exact                   Search for project named exactly 'Core'
  dotmr search api -t                         Search for 'api' including test projects
  dotmr search 'DotMR.C*'                     Search using glob pattern
  dotmr search -r 'DotMR\.(Core|Graph)'      Search using regex pattern
  dotmr search 'DotMR.*' --exclude '*.Tests'  Search with exclusion pattern
  dotmr search core --sort references -d      Sort by reference count descending
  dotmr search --show-history                 Show recent searches

commands.payload:
Generate and manage solution filters (.slnf)

Usage: dotmr slnf <command> [options]

Commands:
  create    Generate a solution filter file

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help information

Use 'dotmr slnf <command> --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Generate a solution filter file

Usage: dotmr slnf create [options]

Options:
  -p, --project <path>    Project to filter for (required)
  -m, --mode <mode>       Filter mode: 'deps', 'dependants'/'dependents', or 'both' (default: both)
  -o, --output <path>     Output path for the .slnf file
  -h, --help              Show help information

Filter modes:
  deps                Include only dependencies (projects the specified project depends on)
  dependants/dependents   Include only dependants (projects that depend on the specified project)
  both                Include full transitive closure (both dependencies and dependants)

Examples:
  dotmr slnf create --project src/MyApp/MyApp.csproj
  dotmr slnf create --project src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --mode deps
  dotmr slnf create -p src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj -m dependants -o myfilter.slnf
  dotmr slnf create -p src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj -m dependents -o myfilter.slnf

commands.payload:
Generate a solution filter file

Usage: dotmr slnf create [options]

Options:
  -p, --project <path>    Project to filter for (required)
  -m, --mode <mode>       Filter mode: 'deps', 'dependants'/'dependents', or 'both' (default: both)
  -o, --output <path>     Output path for the .slnf file
  -h, --help              Show help information

Filter modes:
  deps                Include only dependencies (projects the specified project depends on)
  dependants/dependents   Include only dependants (projects that depend on the specified project)
  both                Include full transitive closure (both dependencies and dependants)

Examples:
  dotmr slnf create --project src/MyApp/MyApp.csproj
  dotmr slnf create --project src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --mode deps
  dotmr slnf create -p src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj -m dependants -o myfilter.slnf
  dotmr slnf create -p src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj -m dependents -o myfilter.slnf

result.stdout:
Run tests for projects

Usage: dotmr test [options]

Options:
  -a, --affected                   Test only affected projects based on git changes
  -b, --base <ref>                 Base git reference for affected detection. Default: HEAD
  -c, --configuration <conf>       Build configuration (Debug, Release, etc.)
  -j, --parallel <count>           Maximum number of parallel test tasks
  --plan                           Show execution plan without running tests
  --no-build                       Skip building before running tests

Coverage Options:
  --coverage                       Collect code coverage
  --coverage-format <format>       Coverage format (cobertura, opencover, lcov, json)

Test Result Options:
  --logger <format>                Test result logger (trx, junit, html)
  --results-directory <dir>        Test results output directory

Sharding Options:
  --shard-index <index>            Shard index (0-based, use with --shard-count)
  --shard-count <count>            Total number of shards (use with --shard-index)

Retry Options:
  --retry <count>                  Number of retry attempts for failed tests

Output Options:
  -q, --quiet                      Suppress progress output
  -v, --verbose                    Show verbose progress output

  -h, --help                       Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr test                                                    # Run all tests
  dotmr test --affected --base main                             # Run tests for affected projects
  dotmr test --coverage --coverage-format cobertura             # Run tests with coverage
  dotmr test --shard-index 0 --shard-count 4                    # Run tests in shard 1 of 4
  dotmr test --logger trx --results-directory TestResults       # Run tests with TRX output
  dotmr test --retry 2                                          # Retry failed tests up to 2 times

commands.payload:
Run tests for projects

Usage: dotmr test [options]

Options:
  -a, --affected                   Test only affected projects based on git changes
  -b, --base <ref>                 Base git reference for affected detection. Default: HEAD
  -c, --configuration <conf>       Build configuration (Debug, Release, etc.)
  -j, --parallel <count>           Maximum number of parallel test tasks
  --plan                           Show execution plan without running tests
  --no-build                       Skip building before running tests

Coverage Options:
  --coverage                       Collect code coverage
  --coverage-format <format>       Coverage format (cobertura, opencover, lcov, json)

Test Result Options:
  --logger <format>                Test result logger (trx, junit, html)
  --results-directory <dir>        Test results output directory

Sharding Options:
  --shard-index <index>            Shard index (0-based, use with --shard-count)
  --shard-count <count>            Total number of shards (use with --shard-index)

Retry Options:
  --retry <count>                  Number of retry attempts for failed tests

Output Options:
  -q, --quiet                      Suppress progress output
  -v, --verbose                    Show verbose progress output

  -h, --help                       Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr test                                                    # Run all tests
  dotmr test --affected --base main                             # Run tests for affected projects
  dotmr test --coverage --coverage-format cobertura             # Run tests with coverage
  dotmr test --shard-index 0 --shard-count 4                    # Run tests in shard 1 of 4
  dotmr test --logger trx --results-directory TestResults       # Run tests with TRX output
  dotmr test --retry 2                                          # Retry failed tests up to 2 times

result.stdout:
ℹ Validate monorepo structure and policies
ℹ 
ℹ Usage: dotmr validate [options]
ℹ 
ℹ Options:
ℹ   -d, --directory <path>    Directory to validate (default: current directory)
ℹ   -f, --auto-fix            Automatically fix violations where possible
ℹ   -p, --policy <id>         Run a specific policy by ID
ℹ   --structure-only          Run only basic structure validation (legacy mode)
ℹ   -h, --help                Show help information
ℹ 
ℹ Available Policies:
ℹ   directory-structure       - Validates that the monorepo follows the recommended directory structure with src/, tests/, and docs/ directories
ℹ   naming-convention         - Validates that projects and directories follow consistent naming conventions (PascalCase for C# projects)
ℹ   solution-file             - Validates that the monorepo has a solution file (.slnx or .sln) at the root
ℹ   directory-build           - Validates that Directory.Build.props and Directory.Build.targets are present and properly configured
ℹ   sdk-version               - Validates that a global.json file exists and pins the .NET SDK version
ℹ   analyzer-consistency      - Validates that analyzer packages and configurations are consistent across all projects
ℹ 
ℹ Examples:
ℹ   dotmr validate                           # Validate with all policies
ℹ   dotmr validate --auto-fix                # Validate and auto-fix issues
ℹ   dotmr validate --policy sdk-version      # Run specific policy
ℹ   dotmr validate --structure-only          # Run basic validation only
ℹ   dotmr validate -d /path/to/repo          # Validate specific directory

result.stdout:
Generate interactive visualizations of the project dependency graph

Usage: dotmr viz [options]

Options:
  -o, --output <path>             Output file path (default: graph.<format>)
  -d, --output-directory <path>   Export all formats to the specified directory
  -f, --format <format>           Output format: html, dot, markdown, json, mermaid, svg (default: html)
  -t, --title <title>             Visualization title

Layout Options:
  --hierarchical                  Use hierarchical layout (HTML/SVG only)
  --no-physics                    Disable physics simulation (HTML only)
  --full-path                     Show full project paths instead of names

Filtering Options:
  --depth <n>                     Limit dependency depth to show
  --projects <names>              Show only specific projects (comma-separated) and their connections
  --exclude-tests                 Hide test projects from visualization
  --dependencies-only             Show only dependencies (not dependants)
  --dependants-only               Show only dependants (not dependencies)

Appearance Options:
  --theme <theme>                 Theme for HTML: light, dark, high-contrast (default: light)

Other Options:
  --open                          Open the generated file (HTML only)
  -h, --help                      Show help information

Examples:
  dotmr viz                                            Generate interactive HTML visualization
  dotmr viz -f svg                                     Generate SVG without requiring Graphviz
  dotmr viz -d ./output                                Export all formats to ./output directory
  dotmr viz --theme dark --open                        Generate dark theme HTML and open it
  dotmr viz --exclude-tests                            Visualize without test projects
  dotmr viz --depth 3                                  Show only 3 levels of dependencies
  dotmr viz --projects Core,Api                        Show only Core and Api projects and their connections
  dotmr viz --dependencies-only --depth 2              Show 2 levels of dependencies only
  dotmr viz -f dot -o graph.dot --hierarchical        Generate hierarchical Graphviz DOT file

Generate interactive visualizations of the project dependency graph

Usage: dotmr viz [options]

Options:
  -o, --output <path>             Output file path (default: graph.<format>)
  -d, --output-directory <path>   Export all formats to the specified directory
  -f, --format <format>           Output format: html, dot, markdown, json, mermaid, svg (default: html)

  --no-physics                    Disable physics simulation (HTML only)

  --exclude-tests                 Hide test projects from visualization
  --dependencies-only             Show only dependencies (not dependants)
  --dependants-only               Show only dependants (not dependencies)

Appearance Options:
  --theme <theme>                 Theme for HTML: light, dark, high-contrast (default: light)

Other Options:

  -h, --help                      Show help information

Examples:

  dotmr viz -f svg                                     Generate SVG without requiring Graphviz

  dotmr viz --theme dark --open                        Generate dark theme HTML and open it

  dotmr viz --projects Core,Api                        Show only Core and Api projects and their connections
  dotmr viz --dependencies-only --depth 2              Show 2 levels of dependencies only
